name: How long does flooring installation take for a whole house?

mainEntity.name: How long does flooring installation usually take for a whole house in Pacifica or San Mateo County?
mainEntity.text: How long does flooring installation usually take for a whole house in Pacifica or San Mateo County?

acceptedAnswer.text: The timeline for a whole-house flooring project depends on more than square footage. A straightforward install with an empty home and a good subfloor can move quickly, while homes with furniture, stairs, moisture issues, leveling work, or multiple flooring types take much longer. Tile and glued-down products usually take more time than floating floors because of prep and cure time. In coastal communities, extra time is often needed for moisture checks, acclimation, and subfloor repairs. If the old flooring must be removed, expect additional labor and disposal time. Projects in occupied homes also take longer because installers often work room by room and must protect furniture, appliances, and living areas. A realistic estimate should include demolition, prep, delivery, acclimation, installation, trim work, and cleanup. Ask the contractor for a day-by-day schedule and whether the home will be usable each night. That matters more than a rough total number of days.
acceptedAnswer.description: Most whole-house flooring projects take anywhere from a few days to two weeks, depending on the square footage, flooring type, subfloor repairs, furniture moving, and drying time for leveling or adhesives. Complex layouts and stairs usually add extra time.
